title: betula alba f. vulgaris
intro: Betula alba f. vulgaris, also known as white birch, is a tree species belonging to the Betulaceae family, characterized by its white, papery bark and slender branches. The medicinal properties of white birch include its high tannin content, which has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of white birch include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural diuretic. White birch is often prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and can also be used in conjunction with other herbs to create a range of medicinal preparations.
